title: Mercedes-Benz CEO talks EV Strategy, future cars
content:
Ola Källenius is a Swedish business executive who has served as CEO of Mercedes-Benz since 2019, becoming the first non-German to lead the legendary luxury automaker. Kallenius joined Opening Bid Unfiltered with Yahoo Finance Executive Editor Brian Sozzi to chat about the future of the auto industry, his decision to massively increase the AMG brand's presence, leaning into tech-heavy car interiors, and playing a role in a driverless-car future.

Video Transcript

0:00 spk_0

All right. Welcome to a new episode of Opening Bid Unfiltered. I'm Yahoo Finance executive editor Brian Sazi, and I, I am a car guy. I've said this repeatedly on this podcast, no matter who I talk to, and I, um, I got a special guest here, and I am so excited to bring in Ola Kalanius, um, someone who I've talked to a little bit in the past, but, um, the CEO Mercedes Benz, I mean, Ola, good to see you. Um, I'm gonna try so hard not to talk about.My car that I just got, um, but it is from your brand, and good to see you, and what I've, there's so many places where I can go to start this, but I just want to start off with this. Uh, I traded it in a, a four door, uh, luxury, uh, car from another brand to get what I got from your brand, and the attention to detail on your automobiles never ceases to amaze me. Where does this come from?

0:52 spk_1

So Brian, uh, uh, great to be on your show today and good to see you again, and I'm really, really happy that you're also part of the, uh, Mercedes family, uh, but where does the attention to detail come from? This year, we're celebrating the 140th anniversary of the invention of the automobile, and for, for those of you who know the story, it was two inventors from southern Germany. On the one hand, Gottlieb Daimler, and on the other hand.Uh, Karl Benz that back in 1886 said that horse great, uh, but car even greater, and he invented the automobile and, uh, Gottlieb Daimler, um, uh, used to have a saying, the best or nothing. Uh, when he talked about that, it's not just talking about the product itself, he's talking about an attitude that if you are that.This part of Germany is called Swabia. If you're that Swabian engineer with an inner unrest for what's next, you never ever rest on your laurels, and that core DNA of the Mercedes-Benz brand is with us still today. So our engineers, when they do something, they don't do it half baked. Uh, so it's about performance. I know what you're driving. Congratulations, but it's about performance and also solid engineering, something that is built to last.Uh, hang on to that car, give it to your children or grandchildren one day. I'm sure it's gonna be worth more than than it is now. I,

2:18 spk_0

I, I guess, that's kind of like what I'm hoping for, but out of everything this car, you know, I just really, I'm a car geek, um, and it's the interior that ultimately got me, and out of all the buttons and all the switches and how fast it is, and the sound of it.It's the little radio wheel um on the steering wheel that just like captured my heart, it's just no, it's it's easy to use, it just, it all works, like as CEO how do you keep that attention to detail ingrained in the company's culture, after all these years?

2:49 spk_1

So innovation is of course the name of the game for a brand like Mercedes. Not only did we invent the car, we have invented so many new technologies over the decades, uh, but we have one motto, if you will. It's not technology for the sake of technology, it's technology for the sake of humans. So take the thing on the steering wheel that uh even though you have everything high tech, latest digital, supercomput, all of those things, uh, latest generation sensors, what have you.Uh, Gen AI agents already now in the car helping you as a little, little helper or butler in the background doing whatever you can, but some things.You just wanna have something that you touch and feel it needs to feel valuable. The materials need to be real and it needs to be intuitive. So, uh, engineering, I think it was Steve Jobs that said this, uh, design is not just how it looks, it's also how it works. And that thing, engineering things for humans, not technology for the sake of technology, that is something that we follow very, very clearly.And the interior of the car has always been important, but I would argue today it's even more important. We used to think cars outside in. Now in many vehicles we think the car inside out. That is the space. That's your third space. You live there part of the time at least, and it needs to feel like home.

4:10 spk_0

I I'm glad you met. I do live in there a lot of my time. Um, I can't tell you how many times or even in the since I've gotten this car that I've just pulled up on my laptop and uh gotten some, some work done. But actually that brings me up to my other point, and I'm gonna get, I want to get into your career a little bit in the future of autos, but are we, there's so many screens, and I, I caught the new interior of the, uh, I think it was the AMG GT and the whole front as all these screens, is that where, is that the future of the automobile interior? I mean, it's just all these screens.

Thank you. If you, if you put yourself in the driver's seat of that car and you watch carefully on these sneak preview pictures that we sent of the interior.You will see that the cockpit is very driver oriented, so those screens are tilted towards you. So if you want to go into track mode and really use one of those race applications from AMG, it is like you're George Russell, but at the same time you're also George Russell's race engineer with the information that he has on the pit wall driving around the lab. So it's there for a purpose.Uh, but it's also your everyday car, and your everyday car, you wanna have, uh, latest and greatest navigation, uh, you wanna be able to use any one of the music apps in a smooth and intuitive way that you use. Maybe you make a break, maybe you're going for a longer trip and you, uh, uh, charge, even though it's super fast charging on this car. Maybe you wanna watch an episode of your show or any show that you like.Uh, and if you have a passenger, that passenger can also on the screen on the other side, uh, do whatever he or she wants. So, uh, maybe you're traveling with one of your children and that person wants to watch a movie. Maybe you're traveling with a business associate, that person can, you know, with the AirPods have a live video conference on that screen while you're driving. The magic behind AMG is, of course, the origins of AMG is the racetrack. So the two guys that started that company, the A and the N, Alfrecht and Melscher.Uh, they used to say when they uh founded AMG in the late 60s, uh, you can't make a Mercedes-Benz better, but you can make it different. And that was the birth of the performance dimension, the true performance dimension of the Mercedes-Benz brand. It's like I'm listening to you all, and it's like you know my car and my experiences. I just had driving into driving into work this morning. I mean, what are your plans with AMG moving forward?

So anyway, I took this car, uh, uh, only a couple of months ago on our internal Mercedes handling track, kind of small racetrack that we have here an hour south of Stuttgart, and I was driving lap after lap after lap, and I was in this V8 mode.My brain was saying my brain was saying V8. It felt like a V8. The best one comes from us.But before you discard what's happening there, try it out when it comes. It is like a V8 on steroids.

How early do you get to test, test drive some of these cars? And are you, were you the one that ultimately signed off on, on the car that I purchased? You know what I got.

Uh, one of the best parts of my job is getting to drive, uh, early prototypes and being part of the kind of concept phase, but also, also, you know, the engineering phase of vehicles. So yes, we are involved, but I want to take 0.0 credit for the engineering. We have, we have what I consider the best team in the world, both the depth but also the breadth of engineering.And it's like it's in the walls here at Mercedes, uh, uh, those, uh, uh, fine engineers, men and women, they know what they are doing. So yes, the board has a role to play. Yes, we sign off, uh, programs. I mean we're talking billions of dollars of investments. It needs to be business cases. We, we run a business, needless to say.And the very car that you have just bought, uh, yes, I did sign that one

off. Thank you.That's for, if you come to New York, we, I guess you just sign the dashboard cause that's, uh, that is cool stuff. You mentioned, um, you mentioned George Russell earlier on. How, how much inspiration do you pull from the Formula One community and pour it back into, you know, the cars that we may, we may see on the road?

Uh, motor sports has always been part of the DNA of Mercedes-Benz. The Mercedes brand, as being named Mercedes, switching from Daimler to Mercedes, was actually a race car. This was at the beginning of the 20th century in 1901. An Austrian industrialist ordered from Gottlieb Daimler and Wilhel Maybach.A brand new vehicle and he wanted to go to a race in in Nice in southern France. They made this car for him. He won the race. His condition was that the car was named after his daughter, was, which was Mercedes, and the rest is history. Does there, uh, how does their popularity, um, Toto and the Mercedes team, I mean, they're on Netflix, like how does that?How does that drive your business? Is that, do you see that correlation?

21:04 spk_1

That is huge. Uh, in terms of marketing, messaging, whatever you wanna call it, uh, Formula One is the best platform in the world, and, uh, why is it even better than any other marketing, uh, opportunity that you could have as a, as a car manufacturer because it's actually the product that you make. The way the sport has grown, especially in the last 10 years.It is phenomenal and I think the current owners of the overall Formula One sport, uh, which is, which is in American ownership, as I'm sure you know, I think they have done a tremendous, tremendous job of expanding the sport. It has grown significantly in the US.And it's not just things like Drive to Survive that opened it up to a whole new audience that maybe didn't know so much about Formula One, and now they saw the stories behind the races and they got to know the drivers and the technology, so that's, that's fascinating. The F1 movie was, I mean, it was a blockbuster hit, one of the biggest blockbusters of last year, to be honest. And as a hardcore Formula One fan, you know, I, I go in and watch a movie like that and maybe just have a little bit of skepticism as a sports person. Is it gonna meet the standard?And I left the theater and I thought, wow, they nailed it. This was a great movie. So all of these things, uh, they work hand in hand, and Formula One is, is, is a growing sport. It's a sport that is also getting younger. The fan base is getting younger. Uh, the fan base is also getting more female. So more and more of the people that identify as active, uh, uh, Formula One fans are younger and also more women.It pays into our demographics, especially in the US who watches it, many people that are in our buying segments. So I could go on and on and on. Uh, Formula One, is a phenomenal success. Of course you try to win. I'm, I'm happy about the start of this season. George and Kimmy, uh, finishing one too, uh, but as in sports, you can never take anything for granted. I'm like good this thing, it wasn't exciting.Uh, but then I see what Elon's doing with robotaxis, those are starting to hit too. So across the board autonomous drive is happening.But don't think about this as mutually exclusive. If you buy a sports car like you have done, of course you enjoy the driving, you, you want to do that. That's, that's part of the, of the whole story of the fun. We're not going to take that fun away from you, but imagine you have, maybe in the future you have an S-Class, you have worked hard, it's, it's late, you're tired. If you can just push a button or tell the car to drive you home in a relaxed way, why wouldn't you want to have that? So it's not an either or. I think it's an and.But autonomy is happening, and we are at the forefront of that.

25:49 spk_0

Is it, how difficult is it to bring these driverless cars to life? You know, I think so many folks, they see these beautiful photos of these cars driving around on streets with nobody in them, uh, on social media, and they're like, well, yeah, I mean, they're just gonna proliferate. I mean, but this is, I mean, these are real technological challenges of our time to make this happen.

No question, uh, many people can do a demo, but very few people can go all the way, and I think in this case, uh, the AI tools are getting so much more sophisticated and with Gen AI entering into the picture where you can have.True end to end learning software stacks.That literally kind of thinks like a human and learn like a human to to simplify it a bit, but in our case also backed up by a rule-based AI stack in the background uh to make sure that nothing goes wrong, uh, we're just at the beginning of this. This is absolutely happening. It's absolutely happening.

A couple more if I could, you know, this is an interesting time.For the world, you know, the geopolitical tensions are running hot, but it does bring back to the forefront that, you know, with gas prices going back up here, at least in the United States and in other parts of the world, the auto industry has moved away from just making those small, tiny 50 mile a gallon cars. And yeah, I know electric cars exist, but when I was growing up in the late 80s, we had these really tiny cars thatYou know, we're really good on gas. I mean, does this environment or backdrop remind the industry that, you know what, maybe we could make a lot of small cars and make a lot of money doing it, or, or that time has just passed.

27:32 spk_1

So if I, if I reflect back uh at what has happened over the last 30, 40 years, if you think back, my, my, actually my own first car was a, uh, uh, a VW Golf Mark One. So the very first generation.And it's right. If I buy a car in that segment today, uh, I guess it is significantly, significantly bigger than the equivalent car back then. Why? One reason is technology and safety and so on. Of course cars today are so much safer and especially in the case of Mercedes, we set the absolute highest standard that has driven part of the of the size and the growth, but it's consumer preference.Uh, it doesn't matter which market we look at, with very, very few exceptions. Every time when we do market research, we talk to our customers, the trend towards SUVs and so on, and we ask them, uh, what else do you want? Uh, very often it comes, love the car that I have. Can it be a little bit bigger, please? So, we're drifting towards where, where, where the consumer preference is. So I don't see Mercedes going into the tiny car business anytime soon.But at the same time, you're not wasteful with space in engineering. It's about smart engineering. On the electric vehicles, you have actually a little bit of an advantage. You can make a longer wheelbase and shorter overhangs. You need less space in the front maybe for an inline 6 or something, so you can get more footprint on the inside of the car with the same footprint on the outside of the car.So, a lot of the engineering that is going on right now is this thinking, let's let's think the car inside out as opposed to outside in.But I don't see, and certainly not in the United States, any big shift towards going smaller, and if you want something that is energetically more efficient.Uh, the electric motor is the most efficient way of turning energy into motion, so get one of our electric cars.

Yes, we have modernized it and of course there is now 50 years or so of engineering taking it into the future, but we have kept that element of it. please, uh, that would be absolutely amazing. Uh, I look forward to that, and we will talk to you soon. That is it for the latest episode of Opening Bit Unfiltered. Thank you always for watching.

title: Mercedes-Benz CEO Defends Bold EV Strategy
content:
Mercedes-Benz CEO, Ola Källenius, sits down with Yahoo Finance executive editor Brian Sozzi to dive into Mercedes’ “all-of-the-above” strategy, the realities of global EV adoption, and why stepping back from electrification now could be a costly mistake. Källenius also shares how the company is positioning itself for long-term leadership in innovation for both electric and performance vehicles.

Video Transcript

00:00 Speaker A

I look at your competitors over at uh Ford and and General Motors. They've scaled back their EV ambitions, I mean significantly the past 24 months, but it sounds like you're still all in.

00:11 Speaker B

Well, uh if you look at what's happening in the world and Mercedes-Benz is a truly global brand, we serve 150 markets around the world. Uh the three most uh important markets obviously, North America, Europe and China.

00:24 Speaker B

Uh not every market is going zero emission at the same speed.

00:29 Speaker B

But the direction of travel is towards zero emission.

00:33 Speaker B

And in the case of Mercedes-Benz, we have made this decision. We know that for the foreseeable future, minimum the next 10 years, probably longer, it's going to be an all- of- the- above scenario.

00:44 Speaker B

But if you now, in our view, sit back and say, okay, the electric thing, it's happening a little bit slower than maybe most people thought five years ago, let's take a pause and we'll come back to that in a few years from now. I think that would be a strategic mistake for Mercedes-Benz.

01:03 Speaker B

You need to stay in that game. If you miss just one beat in terms of what the electrical development uh and the technological development in that space, I think it's going to be very, very difficult to get back into the game.

01:17 Speaker B

So it's true, the complete Mercedes lineup from S-Class at the top all the way down to the entry models like a CLA or a GLA or in Europe, the A-Class. Yes, we will have electric cars as a choice for our customers.

01:33 Speaker B

But as I said, all of the above, many markets uh will take longer to go electric. And if you have a market that is maybe, I don't know, 25% electric and 75% uh ice, uh would you walk away from that?

01:46 Speaker B

No, as an incumbent, uh no sane business person would do that. So we're upholding both.

01:52 Speaker B

So it's like a, it's like a double play in our case, do both things. So we're in a a heavy investment period uh uh in the history of our brand.

02:00 Speaker B

But I think we're setting ourselves up for a leading innovation and technological position towards the end of this decade and into into the next decade. That is our strategy.

content:

title: Hybrids now account for 1 in 5 U.S. vehicle sales as market shifts
content:
Quick Read

Toyota (TM) has electrified vehicles representing 46.9% of retail sales with forecasts for 4.7 million hybrids in fiscal 2026, while Ford (F) leads in hybrid pickups with the F-150 and Maverick but faces $4.81 billion in annual EV losses; General Motors (GM) absorbed $7.2 billion in EV realignment charges and cut capital expenditures despite a forward P/E of 6x. Hybrid market share surged to 19.7% in Q4 2025 from 11-12% year over year as consumers shift toward fuel efficiency amid oil prices at $64.51 per barrel and move away from full-size vehicles, positioning Toyota to capitalize on infrastructure built since 1997. Have You read The New Report Shaking Up Retirement Plans? Americans are answering three questions and many are realizing they can retire earlier than expected.

"Hybrids now are almost one out of every five vehicles that were sold in the United States in the fourth quarter." That single data point reshapes how you should think about the U.S. auto market right now and which companies are positioned to benefit from it.

The shift has been rapid. Hybrid market share surged from roughly 11-12% to 19.7% year over year in Q4, according to Cox Automotive data. Meanwhile, EV growth has shown little meaningful momentum, and the overall sales rate tells a more cautious story: Q1 2026 is running at 15.6 million units, with March coming in slightly better at 15.8 million. Solid, but below earlier expectations. Q4 2025 topped 16 million units, the best since around 2018.

Consumers are also trading down in vehicle size. Buyers are shifting from full-size vehicles toward mid-size alternatives, driven by pricing and fuel economy concerns. With WTI crude sitting at $64.51 per barrel in February 2026 — well below the 2022 peak but still elevated enough to make mpg matter at the dealership — hybrids offer a practical hedge that pure ICE vehicles simply can't match.

Have You read The New Report Shaking Up Retirement Plans? Americans are answering three questions and many are realizing they can retire earlier than expected.

Toyota: Built for This Moment

Toyota (NYSE:TM) has been preparing for this moment since 1997. The Prius was a 30-year bet that hybrid technology would eventually become mainstream. That bet is paying off. Electrified vehicles now represent 46.9% of Toyota's retail sales, and the company forecasts electrified vehicles reaching 49.8% of total sales in fiscal 2026, with 4.7 million hybrids expected. Inventory turnover on core hybrid models is running at five days — that signals a genuine shortage.

Story Continues

Ford: The ICE Boom That Wasn't

Ford Motor Company (NYSE:F) has real hybrid assets. The F-150 and Maverick were the two best-selling hybrid pickup trucks in the U.S. — a genuine competitive advantage. But Ford's EV losses continue to drag: the Model e segment posted a full-year EBIT loss of $4.81 billion. The stock is down 8.8% year to date.

GM: Finding Its Footing

General Motors (NYSE:GM) absorbed over $7.2 billion in special charges from EV capacity realignment and is now cutting capital expenditures aggressively. The Trump-era tailwind for ICE-focused automakers has failed to appear over the last three months. GM's forward guidance of $11.00-$13.00 EPS for 2026 looks credible given a forward P/E of roughly 6x, but the market is waiting for proof.

The hybrid wave is real, durable, and accelerating. Toyota built the infrastructure for it decades ago. Ford has the right truck lineup to compete. GM is still finding its footing. The consumer has already voted with their wallets — nearly one in five vehicles sold last quarter had a hybrid badge on it.

The New Report Shaking Up Retirement Plans

You may think retirement is about picking the best stocks or ETFs and saving as much as possible, but you'd be wrong. After the release of a new retirement income report, wealthy Americans are rethinking their plans and realizing that even modest portfolios can be serious cash machines.

Many are even learning they can retire earlier than expected.

title: GM upgraded at Wolfe Research as firm sees ‘select opportunities’ in autos
content:
Investing.com -- Wolfe Research has upgraded General Motors to Outperform, arguing in a note Wednesday that the recent market sell-off has created attractive entry points across parts of the autos sector.

Analyst Emmanuel Rosner wrote that “auto stocks often are among the main targets when macro concerns escalate,” but history shows such periods “can also present interesting buying opportunities.”

In the past three weeks, the average auto stock has fallen about 8 percent. Wolfe Research said that after updating estimates for production and commodity trends, “the risk/reward profile now appears more attractive for select names.”

The firm lifted GM to Outperform from Peer Perform with a $96 price target, saying investors may be “underappreciating the magnitude of potential tailwinds into 2027.”

Those include a refreshed full-size pickup expected to generate roughly $1.7 billion, lower warranty costs, a reduced tariff burden and “further improved EV losses.” Wolfe Research now models GM earnings of $12.37 per share in 2026 and $16.03 in 2027.

Wolfe also upgraded BorgWarner to Outperform, citing its “Power Gen opportunity,” which could add around $2 billion in revenue at scale. The firm said the stock’s pullback means this upside is not priced in.

Rosner maintained his positive stance on Aptiv ahead of its planned separation, citing “a compelling entry point” and strong fundamentals for both resulting businesses.

By contrast, Wolfe Research flagged execution risk for Ford, citing an unclear 2026 production trajectory and the potential for an overstocked year-end inventory that could create a $1.5 billion EBIT headwind into 2027.

title: Ford vs. Stellantis: Which Automaker Stock Has the Edge Now?
content:
Ford Motor Company F and Stellantis N.V. STLA are two leading automakers in the world. Ford manufactures, markets and services its own cars, trucks, sport utility vehicles, electrified vehicles and Lincoln luxury vehicles, while Stellantis designs, manufactures and markets vehicles under 14 brands. Both companies are recalibrating their strategies to better align with the evolving demand landscape.

Year-to-date, Ford shares plunged 9.8%, while STLA stock is down 38%.Zacks Investment Research

Image Source: Zacks Investment Research

Let us examine the factors that determine why one stock may be more compelling to hold than another.

The Case for Ford

Ford’s revenues rose in 2025 for the fifth straight year, driven by an expanding revenue mix that includes newer segments like hybrid trucks, along with faster growth in higher-margin paid software subscriptions. 

Ford Pro remains a key growth engine, supported by strong order books, rising demand for Super Duty trucks, and expanding software and service offerings. The commercial and fleet business continues to deliver double-digit EBIT margins. Strength across vehicles, software and physical services underpins the segment’s momentum. Paid software subscriptions rose 30% in 2025, with software and physical services profits expected to grow 6.5% this year. Partnership with ServiceTitan enhances digital service capabilities, positioning Ford Pro as a major long-term earnings driver for the company.

Ford’s broad lineup, anchored by its F-Series trucks, Maverick pickup, and popular SUVs, provides a solid foundation for growth. Its hybrid strategy adds resilience as EV adoption evolves. The company is focusing on affordable EVs built on a cost-efficient, universal platform, targeting lower-price models where EV demand in the United States has held up better, and profitability is more achievable.

Ford Energy is another strategic lever, with a $1.5 billion planned investment in 2026 and a goal of reaching 20 GWh of battery storage capacity by 2027. It is a strategic, high-margin growth platform that diversifies revenues beyond traditional auto sales. Ford Energy leverages the company’s manufacturing scale and cost advantage in LFP batteries to enter the fast-growing energy storage market. The company plans to convert part of its Kentucky battery plant with SK On and allocate capacity in Michigan for residential energy storage cells. This initiative monetizes existing battery assets and supports a more resilient, higher-return business model over time.

The legacy automaker has a high dividend yield of more than 4%, way better than the S&P 500’s yield of roughly 1% on average. This high yield could entice those seeking a steady income amid uncertain market conditions.

However, its Model e segment continues to struggle amid stiff competition, pricing pressure and significant costs associated with new-generation EV development. After posting losses of $4.8 billion in 2025, Model e is expected to incur a $4-$4.5 billion loss in 2026. 

The Zacks Consensus Estimate for F’s 2026 EPS has remained unchanged in the past 30 days, while for 2027 EPS it has improved by 4 cents.

Story Continues

Zacks Investment Research

Image Source: Zacks Investment Research

The Case for Stellantis

As of Dec. 31, 2025, the company’s long-term debt rose to €31.8 billion from €25 billion as of Dec. 31, 2024. High leverage limits a firm’s financial flexibility, making it harder to pursue new growth opportunities.

Stellantis intends to invest $13 billion over the next four years to expand its presence in the crucial U.S. market and strengthen its domestic manufacturing base. This marks the largest investment in the company’s 100-year history in the United States and will fund the launch of five new vehicles across its brand portfolio in key segments, as well as the production of a new four-cylinder engine. As a result, it anticipates higher capital expenditures in the coming years, which is expected to put pressure on its cash flows. 

Stellantis is significantly affected by tariffs and other trade barriers imposed by governments across various regions. Recently, there has been a notable increase in tariffs and duties between the United States and its key trading partners, including China, Canada, Mexico, and the European Union. The company relies heavily on imports of vehicles and components from outside the United States, particularly from Canada, Mexico and Italy. Any disruptions in tariff or duty structures could raise costs and negatively impact the availability of raw materials, components and finished vehicles. This, in turn, may lead to higher consumer prices, reduced demand, and pressure on the company’s profitability. The company expects pressures from tariffs and raw materials to remain headwinds in 2026. 

Due to the net loss recorded in 2025, the company has decided not to issue a dividend in 2026, which can disappoint income-focused investors. 

However, Stellantis expects strong gains in 2026 driven by a broad lineup of new products launched in late 2025 targeting unmet market segments, along with additional launches planned for early 2026. The company anticipates mid-single-digit growth in net revenues and a low-single-digit adjusted operating income margin. It also expects performance to improve sequentially from the first half of the year to the second half.

The Zacks Consensus Estimate for STLA’s 2026 and 2027 EPS has declined 43 cents and 49 cents, respectively, in the past 30 days.Zacks Investment Research

Image Source: Zacks Investment Research

Conclusion

Ford benefits from steady revenue growth, a resilient product mix led by hybrids and trucks, and high-margin opportunities through Ford Pro and energy solutions. Its consistent dividend yield further enhances its appeal, particularly in uncertain markets.

By contrast, Stellantis faces rising leverage, significant capital expenditure commitments, and near-term cash flow pressures, alongside the suspension of dividends following its 2025 loss. While its product pipeline may support a recovery, pressures from tariffs and raw materials are likely to hurt profits. 

Overall, Ford’s balanced strategy, income potential and clearer growth trajectory, along with its current Zacks Rank #3 (Hold), make it a more attractive and stable choice compared to Stellantis, which has a Zacks rank #5 (Strong Sell).

title: Ford Has Run Out Of Places To Go
content:
Quick Read

Ford Is Losing Ground In Europe China Sales Are In Trouble Tariffs Help Ford In The US Have You read The New Report Shaking Up Retirement Plans? Americans are answering three questions and many are realizing they can retire earlier than expected.

There are many regions worldwide where cars can be sold. However, without success in China, the EU, and the US, global automotive companies get painted into a corner. Ford’s (NYSE: F) US sales often rank third behind (NYSE: GM) and Toyota (NYSE: TM). Ford makes money in China, but exports are critical to its profit there.  Sales inside China have weakened.

The world has gotten smaller still for Ford. EU sales (registrations) in the first two months of this year fell 21.5% to 41,039. No other global manufacturer posted a drop as severe as that. Given how crowded the market is across the countries in the region, it is impossible to see how Ford can regain its footing.

Have You read The New Report Shaking Up Retirement Plans? Americans are answering three questions and many are realizing they can retire earlier than expected.

Most of the truly global car companies do better than Ford in the EU. In the first two months of the year, Kia registered 60,004 units. Hyundai’s figure dropped 16%, but it still posted registrations of 55,570. Toyota, which has bested Ford in the US, posted registrations of 117,510, off by 6.5%. However, that puts its number at almost three times Ford’s.

Ford has been pushed onto an island, which is its home market. Its US sales rose 6% to 2,204,124 in 2025. It owns the critical full-size pickup market. Its F-Series pick-ups sold 828,832 units, up 8.3% from 2024.

Ford’s fate as a company depends primarily on one thing. The US government has 100% tariffs on Chinese EV imports. And that could stay in place indefinitely. Depending on who is counting, China has provided over $3 billion in financial support to BYD, the country's largest EV company. Across the entire Chinese EV industry, the investment is larger than that. A very reasonable argument is that the US should not let its car industry be crippled by Chinese companies that were given such a large amount of financial help.

For the sake of Ford, China EV tariffs will last for years. Ford has not yet demonstrated that it can be as effective as it works to expand its global sales footprint. At least today, because of tariffs, it has some time.

title: T. ROWE PRICE EXPLORES LEADERSHIP, TRANSFORMATION, AND THE FUTURE OF MOBILITY WITH FORD CEO JIM FARLEY
content:
Newest episode of "The Angle" from T. Rowe Price features a candid conversation on strategic reinvention, electrification realities, and leading with urgency and purpose

BALTIMORE, March 25, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- T. Rowe Price, a global investment management firm and a leader in retirement, announced the latest episode of "The Angle from T. Rowe Price," an investment-themed podcast focused on the forces shaping financial markets and the leaders navigating them.Jim Farley, President & CEO of Ford Motor Company

In the newest episode, host Eric Veiel, Head of Global Investments and Chief Investment Officer for T. Rowe Price, sits down with Jim Farley, President and Chief Executive Officer of Ford Motor Company, one of the world's leading automotive manufacturers, with a global footprint spanning passenger vehicles, commercial trucks, and mobility solutions.

Veiel and Farley explored Farley's leadership journey, from his early passion for cars and two decades at Toyota to joining Ford in 2007 during the onset of the global financial crisis, and how those experiences have shaped his approach to leading one of the world's most iconic industrial companies.

"This conversation highlighted what disciplined leadership looks like in practice," said Veiel. "Jim's experience leading through multiple industry cycles brings important context to today's challenges, and his perspective reflects a leader who values focus, honesty, and learning from what hasn't worked as much as what has."

ABOUT "THE ANGLE"

"The Angle" podcast brings listeners dynamic insights on the forces shaping financial markets, featuring the T. Rowe Price global investing team and special guests. Through engaging conversations, "The Angle from T. Rowe Price" aims to foster curiosity by asking better questions and delivering better insights, allowing investors to gain a deeper understanding of today's evolving market themes.

Launched in 2024, "The Angle" has explored a range of topics, including the artificial intelligence, health care innovation, forward-looking expectations for global markets, key market drivers from the perspectives of some of the world's leading CEOs, and more recently taking a closer look at the future of the energy sector, speaking with experts across T. Rowe Price about the key themes shaping tomorrow's energy landscape.

This is the twelfth episode of T. Rowe Price's C-suite podcast series. The first ten episodes, also available now, featured H. Lawrence Culp, Jr., chairman and CEO of GE Aerospace; Meredith Kopit Levien, president and CEO of The New York Times Company; Gary Guthart, CEO of Intuitive Surgical; Jensen Huang, founder and CEO of NVIDIA Corporation; Darren Woods, chairman and CEO of ExxonMobil; Harvey Schwartz, Chief Executive Officer and Director, and David Rubenstein, Co-Founder and Co-Chairman of the Board of Carlyle; Jane Fraser, CEO of Citi; Sarah Friar, CFO of OpenAI; Dave Ricks, CEO of Eli Lilly; and Srini Gopalan, CEO of T-Mobile. "The Angle from T. Rowe Price" is available across multiple platforms, including Spotify and Apple Podcasts. Future episodes will be announced as they are produced. For more information on the podcast please click here.

Story Continues

title: This Could Cut Tesla’s Stock Price By 70%
content:
Quick Read

Optimus May Not Work Some Recovery In Europe Revenue Still From Cars Have You read The New Report Shaking Up Retirement Plans? Americans are answering three questions and many are realizing they can retire earlier than expected.

What if Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) is primarily a car company?

After months of being beaten down in Europe, Tesla’s sales are making a comeback, where both competition and Elon Musk's political stances had hurt it last year. In February, EU sales jumped 29% to 13,740. However, it lagged rival BYD, which saw sales increase 185% to 15,438 for the month.

Have You read The New Report Shaking Up Retirement Plans? Americans are answering three questions and many are realizing they can retire earlier than expected.

Questions remain about whether Tesla can reverse its falling EV sales. It continues to struggle in China, the world’s largest EV market, where dozens of EV brands compete. In the US, the entire EV industry has been hurt by the end of the $7,500 tax credit for EV purchases, which expired on September 30. However, Tesla may be fortunate that the companies that tried to take its market share in America have largely retreated from the business and taken large write-offs. This includes Ford  (NYSE: F) and GM (NYSE: GM). (A large inventory of used Teslas in the US could undermine sales of its new vehicles.)

Tesla’s market cap does not rely primarily on its EV sales, and that may harm investors sooner rather than later.

Tesla’s Robotaxi has been accident-prone. Additionally, several accidents involving its legacy cars have caused injuries. Therefore, there are questions about the safety of the design of some vehicles. Google’s Waymo has challenged Tesla with its own self-driving vehicles, and, in China, a company called WeRide is a rival.  And, its Opimus robot may never become mainstream at all. It has competitors as well. This includes Agility Robotics (Digit) and Boston Dynamics (Atlas).

Suppose Tesla’s value became more dependent on car sales because some of its new initiatives faltered? Elon Musk says these initiatives are the cornerstone of its market cap.

So, what if Tesla turns out to be primarily an EV company? Its current market cap is $1.43 trillion. The market cap of BYD, the world's largest EV company, is $144 billion. Toyota, the world's largest car company by unit sales, has a market cap of $274 billion. This means if the market views Tesla primarily as a car company, its market cap would crater.

It is hard to find anyone who will make the case for a major Tesla market-cap reset today. It is worth noting, however, that its stock price has not done much better than the S&P 500 over the last five years. The S&P is up 66% over the period. Tesla shares are up 84% over that period. It has had two major downturns along the way, the first in early 2023 and the second in March and April next year.

Story Continues

Investors have been patient as Musk has made his robot, AI, and self-driving cars. If one or more of them fail, his argument begins to erode.

The New Report Shaking Up Retirement Plans

You may think retirement is about picking the best stocks or ETFs and saving as much as possible, but you'd be wrong. After the release of a new retirement income report, wealthy Americans are rethinking their plans and realizing that even modest portfolios can be serious cash machines.

Many are even learning they can retire earlier than expected.

If you're thinking about retiring or know someone who is, take 5 minutes to learn more here.

title: Woodchuck Partners with Walbridge to Help Advance Customers' Construction Sustainability Goals
content:
Early results show major gains in tracking waste, reducing disposal, cutting costs and improving efficiency at Ford construction project in Marshall, Michigan

GRAND RAPIDS, Mich., March 24, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Woodchuck, the AI-powered climate-tech startup redefining how construction and manufacturing industries handle wood waste, today announced a joint sustainability initiative with Walbridge, one of the nation's top industrial and automotive constructors. The program supports Ford Motor Company's construction waste-reduction efforts at its new manufacturing facility in Marshall, Mich.Woodchuck, an AI-powered climate-tech startup, is redefining how construction and manufacturing industries handle wood waste,

In the first three months, the program has given teams a clearer view of the materials being discarded, diversion rates, cost reductions, and operational efficiency — already achieving 40% of the project's projected materials-related savings. This early progress offers Walbridge a powerful solution to address customers' waste management needs and lays the groundwork for a new standard operating procedure for future large-scale construction projects.

A Legacy Builder Confronts a Modern Waste Challenge

For more than a century, Walbridge has delivered some of the most complex automotive and industrial projects in North America. As Walbridge's customers expand their sustainability commitments, construction waste management is a growing priority — particularly on megaprojects where the volume and variability of materials can shift daily.

On the Ford project, wood waste quickly emerged as one of the most unpredictable waste elements. Crating, dunnage, international shipping pallets, and custom rigging arrived in wide-ranging sizes and material types, creating a diverse and constantly changing waste stream.

These complexities revealed opportunities for innovation. Walbridge saw the potential to elevate efficiency, reduce hauling expenses, and strengthen alignment with Ford's sustainability goals. The need for real-time visibility into container levels and the makeup of each load became a catalyst for adopting a smarter, data-driven solution — one that made waste handling more predictable, cost-effective and sustainable.

"Our partnership with Woodchuck is built on collaboration. Transparent and real-time communication allows our team to adapt quickly to changing material waste streams on the ground. Detailed information about each load provides complete visibility not only into what is diverted from a landfill, but also into its end destination and intended use, delivering transparency and enabling measurable sustainability outcomes," said Sander Mathijs, Walbridge Sustainability Manager. "Another key program feature is its ability to scale, allowing us to calibrate capacity and scope to meet the waste‑diversion needs of the project."

Story Continues

Woodchuck's AI Platform Delivers Immediate, Scalable Impact

Woodchuck.ai leverages its AI platform across the Ford project to track, report and validate the diversion of wood, cardboard, plastic, and metal; all with minimal onsite labor and seamless integration into Walbridge's existing workflows.

Walbridge saw meaningful improvements within the first quarter diverting thousands of tons of wood, cardboard, plastic and metal; reducing waste, reducing landfill dependency, and reducing costs. Over the course of the project, Woodchuck will divert 8,000 tons of wood and 1,000 tons of cardboard, plastic, and metal from landfills.

Woodchuck's detailed reporting also strengthens accountability, giving Walbridge clear data documenting recycling and reuse for both internal tracking and customer sustainability documentation.

Because the Woodchuck platform is designed for large, multi-phase construction programs, the improvements seen at the Marshall project can be replicated at scale. Whether deployed on a single megaproject or rolled out across multiple sites, contractors gain the same visibility, control, and cost efficiencies, making the solution a powerful model for nationwide waste management and sustainability performance.

"Our partnership with Woodchuck has been a game-changer," said Ross Linton, Group Vice President, Walbridge. "In just a few short months, they've helped us transform our waste process to one that's measurable, trackable, and easily managed. Our team is empowered to plan ahead, driving efficiency and sustainability. We're excited about the future possibilities this collaboration brings."

Creating a New Standard for Future Walbridge Projects

Based on early results, Walbridge expects the Woodchuck-enabled process to become a foundation for future large-scale builds across automotive, manufacturing, technology, and advanced industrial sectors.

"Walbridge is demonstrating what it looks like when a contractor treats waste as a strategic input rather than an afterthought," said Todd Thomas, CEO of Woodchuck. "By embracing real-time data, AI-enabled insights, and a commitment to measurable sustainability outcomes, they're proving that smarter waste management isn't just good for the environment — it's good for productivity, cost efficiency, and project certainty. Their leadership on Ford's Marshall project shows what's possible when innovation becomes part of the construction workflow, and they're setting the pace for how the industry will operate going forward."

About Woodchuck
Woodchuck is a climate impact start-up dedicated to empowering contractors, manufacturers, and biomass energy producers by streamlining wood waste diversion and processing. We are committed to leveraging advanced AI technologies to transform waste into valuable resources, reduce landfill usage, and provide a steady, sustainable supply of biomass. Based in Grand Rapids, Michigan, Woodchuck is funded by an investor syndicate led by Mason Fink, Beckett Industries, NorthStar Clean Energy and Alloy Partners. For more information, visithttps://woodchuck.ai/.

About Walbridge Walbridge is one of America's largest privately held construction companies founded in Detroit in 1916. The company offers construction management, engineering, and real estate services for customers in manufacturing, hyperscale data centers, automotive, defense, higher education, health care, and government. Walbridge employs more than 1,500 professionals in North America. Visitwww.walbridge.com or connect with us onLinkedIn to learn more.Early results show major gains in tracking waste, reducing disposal, cutting costs and improving efficiency at Ford construction project in Marshall, Michigan.Cision

title: EU car sales rebound in February, EVs capture rising share
content:
[Dealership offered various finance options, including an auto loan or lease, making it easier to buy or rent a car from the company. Agent reviewed contract, finalizing insurance agreement.]
Phiwath Jittamas/iStock via Getty Images

EU passenger car registrations showed modest recovery in February 2026, rising 1.4% Y/Y to 865,437 units, rebounding from a 3.9% decline in January as demand improved across key market.

Among the bloc's major markets, growth was led by strong performances in Germany, where registrations rose 3.8%, alongside a 7.5% gain in Spain and 14% in Italy. However, France remained a notable laggard, with registrations falling 14.7%.

Year-to-date through February, registrations slipped 1.2%, but the battery-electric vehicle (BEV) segment stole the show. Battery-electric cars accounted for 18.8% of the EU market share in January-February 2026, an increase from 15.2% one year earlier. February BEV registrations surged 20.6% to 158,280 units, led by strong gains in Germany, up 28.7%, France climbed 27.8%, Denmark scaled 26.1%, and Italy jumped a whopping 81.3%.

YTD in the EU, Chinese automaker BYD Company (BYDDY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BYDDY]) sales saw the biggest jump of 179% to 29,291 units, while Tesla (TSLA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TSLA]) sales climbed 17% to 20,941 units. Volvo (VLKAF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VLKAF]), Ford (F [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/F]), Suzuki (SZKMY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SZKMY]), and Mitsubishi (MSBHF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MSBHF]) saw a drop in the same period.

Tickers to watch: Volkswagen Group (OTCPK:VLKAF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VLKAF]), Stellantis (STLA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/STLA]), Renault Group (OTCPK:RNSDF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RNSDF]), Hyundai (OTCPK:HYMTF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HYMTF]), Toyota Motor (NYSE:TM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TM]), BMW (BMWYY), Mercedes-Benz (OTCPK:MBGAF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MBGAF]), Ford Motor (NYSE:F [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/F]), Volvo Cars, Tesla (TSLA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TSLA]), Nissan (OTCPK:NSANY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NSANY]), and Suzuki (OTCPK:SZKMY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SZKMY]).

title: Are Any of These Tesla Competitors Buys in 2026?
content:
Key Points

Rivian is partnering with Uber in a deal worth $1.25 billion. BYD surpassed Tesla in global sales last year. Ford and GM have long-term plans to build their EV market share.These 10 stocks could mint the next wave of millionaires ›

Tesla(NASDAQ: TSLA) is navigating a complex electric vehicle landscape amid increasingly fierce competition. Pure-play EV makers such as Rivian(NASDAQ: RIVN) and BYD(OTC: BYDDY) are gaining global traction and brand recognition, while legacy automakers like Ford Motor Company(NYSE: F) and General Motors(NYSE: GM) continue their pivot into electric options.

Which of the biggest Tesla competitors are buys, though? Let's take a look at what's happening among the main EV manufacturers.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

Rivian's inflection point is here

Rivian has garnered some positive headlines recently as the innovative EV maker prepares to release its high-tech, yet affordable, R2 fleet this spring. Rivian is also partnering with Uber to provide 50,000 self-driving taxis. The deal means Uber will invest $1.25 billion into the company over the next five years.

Rivian's automotive division is still not profitable, but a joint venture with Volkswagen is providing the business with a meaningful cash cushion. Because of the technology services Rivian provides to Volkswagen, the company has a positive overall gross profit.

Image source: Getty Images.

Rivian needs the R2 launch and Uber partnership to succeed and create a real path to profitability that isn't solely dependent on its engineering and technology services. Rivian is still speculative, but definitely trending in the right direction. The upcoming launch of its mid-size mass-market SUVs is an important milestone.

Rivian remains a high-risk, high-reward speculative buy for investors willing to tolerate volatility.

BYD overtakes Tesla

The Chinese-based automaker BYD surpassed Tesla last year as the world's best-selling EV maker. The company is almost fully vertically integrated, building its own batteries, semiconductors, and most components. This structure gives it a very real competitive advantage.

BYD is rapidly expanding globally, opening factories in Southeast Asia, Europe, and Latin America. The biggest issue BYD is facing right now is back home in China. The EV maker's sales in its homeland have declined steadily as formidable competition erodes its market share.

Because BYD is based in China, for U.S. investors, this does pose a geopolitical risk. The company needs to prove it can be sustainably profitable overseas while also clawing back market share in China. The stock has fallen nearly 27% over the past year, so for patient investors, BYD is fairly priced.

Ford's successful pivot

Ford is obviously not a pure-play EV manufacturer. The legacy automaker's push into EVs has been far more successful than those of other combustion-engine manufacturers. Still, the company's line of EVs is struggling to build strong momentum in the U.S.

Ford's EV models are affordable and competitive with other brands such as Tesla. The Michigan-based company also has a diverse blend of vehicles, including hybrids, which creates a more reliable cash flow. The company's EV roadmap isn't as exciting as Tesla's or Rivian's, but its track record is solid.

Because Ford's fleet is a mix of combustion, hybrid, and EV models, it has a larger moat and is more resistant to downturns. EV sales have slowed, but Ford is still in a stronger overall position than the pure-play EV makers.

Ford's stock is better suited to value investors than to growth investors. The company's dividend yield is a whopping 5.15% right now. Ford stock has declined 12% to start 2026. EVs are not a bright spot for Ford at the moment. If you're going to invest in Ford as an EV play, you'll need to expect a long time horizon when it comes to finding its footing in the space.

General Motors is underappreciated

GM is a lot like Ford in that it's a legacy automaker with a wide array of gas, hybrids, and electric vehicles. GM is also investing in software and is seeing a scalable revenue boost because of it. With both software and cash-printing gas vehicles, GM's EV fleet is less risky.

GM's stock has had a great 12 months, rising more than 47% as of March 20. The company's forward price-to-earnings ratio is also currently under 6. This figure is low even in an industry known for its very reasonable P/E ratios.

What stock should you buy?

All of the Tesla competitors discussed here have different reasons why they might be attractive to investors. Ford and GM are well-established and profitable, and thus slightly less risky. You likely won't see exciting growth with them, though. Rivian and BYD could potentially dethrone Tesla worldwide as they continue to expand their brands. They are, however, far more speculative. Which competitor stock to choose depends on your goals and risk tolerance, but each company makes its own bullish case.

Don’t miss this second chance at a potentially lucrative opportunity

Ever feel like you missed the boat in buying the most successful stocks? Then you’ll want to hear this.

On rare occasions, our expert team of analysts issues a “Double Down” stock recommendation for companies that they think are about to pop. If you’re worried you’ve already missed your chance to invest, now is the best time to buy before it’s too late. And the numbers speak for themselves:

Nvidia:if you invested $1,000 when we doubled down in 2009,you’d have $447,961!*Apple: if you invested $1,000 when we doubled down in 2008, you’d have $47,222!*Netflix: if you invested $1,000 when we doubled down in 2004, you’d have $495,179!*

title: Ford Motor Company (F) Surpasses Market Returns: Some Facts Worth Knowing
content:
Ford Motor Company (F) closed at $11.76 in the latest trading session, marking a +2.08% move from the prior day. The stock's change was more than the S&P 500's daily gain of 1.15%. Meanwhile, the Dow gained 1.38%, and the Nasdaq, a tech-heavy index, added 1.38%.

Shares of the company witnessed a loss of 17.77% over the previous month, trailing the performance of the Auto-Tires-Trucks sector with its loss of 11.9%, and the S&P 500's loss of 5.69%.

The upcoming earnings release of Ford Motor Company will be of great interest to investors. The company's earnings per share (EPS) are projected to be $0.22, reflecting a 57.14% increase from the same quarter last year. Meanwhile, our latest consensus estimate is calling for revenue of $39.56 billion, up 5.71% from the prior-year quarter.

Looking at the full year, the Zacks Consensus Estimates suggest analysts are expecting earnings of $1.52 per share and revenue of $175.03 billion. These totals would mark changes of +39.45% and +0.57%, respectively, from last year.

Additionally, investors should keep an eye on any recent revisions to analyst forecasts for Ford Motor Company. These latest adjustments often mirror the shifting dynamics of short-term business patterns. title: Stock Market Today, March 23: Tesla Rises on Joint Venture Plans to Build a Semiconductor Plant for AI Chip Production
content:
EV maker Tesla(NASDAQ:TSLA) closed Monday at $380.83, up 3.50%. The stock moved higher as investors weighed fresh analyst downgrades and delivery cuts against news surrounding Tesla’s artificial intelligence (AI) strategy.
Trading volume reached 72.6 million shares, coming in nearly 18% above its three-month average of 61.3 million shares. Tesla IPO'd in 2010 and has grown 23,852% since going public.

How the markets moved today

The S&P 500(SNPINDEX:^GSPC) advanced 1.16% to finish at 6,582, while the Nasdaq Composite(NASDAQINDEX:^IXIC) climbed 1.38% to close at 21,947. Among automobile manufacturing peers, Ford Motor Company(NYSE:F) closed at $11.76 (+2.08%) and General Motors(NYSE:GM) finished at $75.72 (+4.00%) as investors assessed sector safety and demand trends.

What this means for investors

Over the weekend, CEO Elon Musk revealed the Terafab initiative, outlining plans for a semiconductor manufacturing facility. This project will be a collaboration between Tesla, xAI, and SpaceX, with the early phases projected to require an investment in the tens of billions of dollars.

Investors balanced those future plans with an analyst downgrade and $150 price target. Trip Chowdry with Global Equitites Research cited concerns over its AI strategy for the downgrade. Analysts at UBS(NYSE:UBS) also cut Tesla delivery forecasts and reports flagged mounting regulatory probes.

Many investors still want to own Tesla stock based on future potential and plans touted by Musk. Weakness in the EV business itself hasn’t been what is driving its shares. That looked to be true again today.

title: Ford’s MLB Deal And Home Energy Pilot Shape Long Term Story
content:
Get insights on thousands of stocks from the global community of over 7 million individual investors at Simply Wall St.

Ford Motor (NYSE:F) has become the new Official Automotive Partner of Major League Baseball under an exclusive multiyear agreement, replacing Chevrolet after roughly two decades in that role. The partnership extends across MLB, Minor League, and Little League programming, with co-branded campaigns planned around league events and fan engagement. Separately, Ford is participating in a Puget Sound Energy pilot that uses EVs, including the F 150 Lightning, as backup power sources for homes through vehicle to home technology.

For investors watching NYSE:F, these developments sit at the intersection of brand reach and product capability. MLB exposure gives Ford broad national visibility across different age groups and fan segments, while the Puget Sound Energy pilot highlights how its EV portfolio can plug into real world energy use cases.

Both moves reflect areas where the auto industry is putting attention, from high profile sponsorships to grid connected EVs and home energy resilience. As these partnerships progress, you can track how Ford incorporates them into marketing, product positioning, and any future offerings it may develop that are tied to energy services.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Ford Motor by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Ford Motor.NYSE:F Earnings & Revenue Growth as at Mar 2026

📰 Beyond the headline: 2 risks and 4 things going right for Ford Motor that every investor should see.

The MLB partnership and the Puget Sound Energy vehicle to home pilot pull Ford in two complementary directions: brand reach and technology credibility. On the marketing side, replacing Chevrolet as MLB’s Official Automotive Partner gives Ford a fresh platform in front of a national audience, from televised marquee events like the World Series to community focused Little League programs. That kind of exposure can help keep Ford’s truck and SUV line up top of mind against rivals such as General Motors and Stellantis.

The V2H pilot with Puget Sound Energy, ChargeScape, Kia and Wallbox speaks more to product capability and long term positioning of Ford’s EVs. Using the F 150 Lightning as a home backup and grid resource lines up with themes in Ford’s broader push into software, connected data and energy related services. For you as an investor, the interest is less about near term volumes and more about whether these projects build defensible use cases that support Ford’s EV and software ambitions over time.

Story Continues

How This Fits Into The Ford Motor Narrative

The V2H pilot aligns with the narrative around connected vehicles and software driven services by showing another way Ford can use battery capacity and data to support recurring revenue opportunities in Ford Pro and beyond. Heavy reliance on legacy ICE trucks and SUVs remains a key concern in the narrative, and an MLB sponsorship focused on those vehicles could reinforce that dependence if EV and hybrid marketing does not keep pace. The specific grid services angle of using EVs as distributed storage is not fully covered in the narrative, which focuses more on telematics and over the air software, so this program could add an extra layer to the long term story.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story.  Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Ford Motor to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

⚠️ Execution risk around EV programs remains, including the need to manage Model e losses and deliver products that can compete on cost and features with Tesla, BYD and other global automakers. ⚠️ Analysts have flagged that Ford’s debt is not well covered by operating cash flow, so large marketing commitments or new technology investments could matter for future balance sheet flexibility. 🎁 The MLB partnership can reinforce brand strength for trucks and commercial vehicles, which ties into the narrative that Ford’s core business and Ford Pro segment are important earnings pillars. 🎁 The V2H pilot may support Ford’s push into software and energy services, potentially helping the company make better use of its connected vehicle data and charging partnerships over time.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, it is worth watching how Ford uses MLB events to highlight specific products, including hybrids and EVs, and whether those campaigns show up in future comments about demand mix. On the V2H side, the key signals will be customer feedback, utility interest and any mention of scaling similar programs beyond Puget Sound Energy once the pilot data is in. Updates on Ford Pro software offerings, tariff related cost pressures and progress on the EV reset will also help show how these partnerships fit into the broader mix of marketing spend, technology bets and balance sheet priorities.

title: Ford Motor Company Announces Details for Q1 2026 Earnings Conference Call
content:
DEARBORN, Mich., March 23, 2026--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Ford Motor Company and Ford Motor Credit Company will release first-quarter 2026 financial results at 4:05 p.m. ET on Wednesday, Apr. 29.

At 5:00 p.m. ET the same day, Jim Farley, president and chief executive officer; Sherry House, chief financial officer; and other members of the Ford senior management team will host a conference call to discuss the results and the company’s progress in delivering its ambitious Ford+ plan for growth and value creation. Representatives of the investment community will be able to ask questions during the call.

The presentation and supporting material will be available at https://shareholder.ford.com.

Ford Earnings Webcast– Wednesday, Apr. 29, at 5 p.m. ET

Replay – Available after 8 p.m. ET Wednesday, Apr. 29 through Wednesday, May 6

Registration beforehand is strongly recommended to expedite access to the call

Webcast: https://ford-motor-company-q1-2026-earnings-call.open-exchange.net/

About Ford Motor Company

Ford Motor Company (NYSE: F) is a global company based in Dearborn, Michigan, committed to helping build a better world, where every person is free to move and pursue their dreams. The company’s Ford+ plan for growth and value creation combines existing strengths, new capabilities, and always-on relationships with customers to enrich experiences for customers and deepen their loyalty. Ford develops and delivers innovative, must-have Ford trucks, sport utility vehicles, commercial vans and cars and Lincoln luxury vehicles, along with connected services, including BlueCruise (ADAS) and security. The company offers freedom of choice through three customer-centered business segments: Ford Blue, engineering iconic gas-powered and hybrid vehicles; Ford Model e, inventing breakthrough electric vehicles ("EVs") along with embedded software that defines always-on digital experiences for all customers; and Ford Pro, helping commercial customers transform and expand their businesses with vehicles and services tailored to their needs. Additionally, the Company provides financial services through Ford Motor Credit Company. Ford employs about 169,000 people worldwide. More information about the company and its products and services is available at corporate.ford.com.

title: Formula 1 CEO on shorter races, Max Verstappen, new regulations, and Apple
content:
Listen and subscribe to Opening Bid Unfiltered on Apple Podcasts, Amazon Music, Spotify, YouTube, or wherever you find your favorite podcasts.

Formula One CEO Stefano Domenicali is a legend in the world of auto sports.

I've known the super-energetic Italian-born Domenicali since 2017, when he was CEO of supercar maker Lamborghini. If you drive a $300,000 Urus SUV and are pleased that Lamborghini still uses powerful gas engines, he is the guy to thank.

Interestingly, the travel warrior doesn't have a garage full of cars, as you might expect.

"I don't drive, and I don't have a car," he said. "I had a Urus when I was at Lamborghini. But now, in London, how you can drive, I mean, it takes more time to find a parking spot than I stay in the office."

After rising through the ranks at Ferrari, Domenicali served as its team principal from 2008 to 2014, notably leading the Scuderia to a Constructors' Championship in 2008.

Following his transformative stint as CEO of Lamborghini, he took the reins as CEO of Formula One in 2021.

In his current role, Domenicali has been the chief architect of F1's global expansion, successfully navigating the sport into a record-breaking 24-race calendar, spearheading the massive 2026 technical regulations reset, and pushing deeper into the US market.

The F1 brand has gotten a huge lift from the Netflix (NFLX) show "Drive to Survive" and ever-increasing corporate sponsorships. Now, with General Motors' (GM) Cadillac brand added to the grid and a new deal with Apple (AAPL) to broadcast the races, Domenicali has his work cut out to keep the growth hot.

"The beauty of what we are doing is that we are able to capture the interest of the young fans audience in a very quick time, because I always do believe that when we're talking about entertainment, we need to activate the five senses. If we are not able to activate the five senses, we are losing something," Domenicali said of the F1 brand's broadening appeal.

Domenicali — who recently inked a new five-year contract — shared his perspective on several of the sport's hottest topics in our 30-minute chat.Formula One Group CEO Stefano Domenicali, right, on the grid with a dignitary during the F1 Grand Prix of Australia at Albert Park Grand Prix Circuit on March 8, 2026, in Melbourne, Australia. (Mark Sutton - Formula 1/Formula 1 via Getty Images)·Mark Sutton - Formula 1 via Getty Images

On whether F1 will stay at 24 races

"I go back to what I said before: It's a matter of quality. We, of course, also have logistics constraints. You can say that we can go even higher. Well, technically yes, but today we do believe that the number 24 is the right dimension that distributes our presence all around the world ... In terms of offerings, in terms of places and countries that would like to host F1, we could easily go much, much higher than the number. But this will enable us to keep the right pressure with our promoters to deliver a better-quality product for the fans."

Story Continues

On whether F1 has finally cracked the US market

"In a way, yes. And on the other way, not enough, because we want to deliver even more in the future. If you just think that a couple of years ago, we had only one race in Austin, that, you know, it was quite difficult to have more than 60,000 people for the weekend ... We now have Miami, and this will be the third year in Las Vegas. And in the blink, now we move in a dimension where, for example, in Austin we have more than 450,000 people. And now we have, you know, an incredible success in Miami that is sold out and in Las Vegas, that is becoming a reference Grand Prix for the world."

On new driving regulations that star Max Verstappen has called a 'joke'

"Well, if you look at the first two races, we received 99.9% [of viewers saying,] 'Wow, how amazing were the races?' Because we had a lot of overtaking that people understand that as a main factor of having fun. Of course, as always, it is normal [to have negative feedback]. That is always been the case when there is a step change of a regulation, and this is very big ... Of course, Max expresses his opinion, which we need to respect. But we take on board the positivity of the fact that he wants to be for one and wants to make sure that this comment is taken on board. We will, and the FIA and the teams will, but we don't have to lose the bigger-picture focus. The fans want to have excitement on the race, want to have a lot of overtaking, want to have action on the track."

On speculation that he wanted to shorten F1 races

"I only said we need to make sure that we connect with the fans to understand if the length of the race is too long or too short. And that's the reason why, for example, instead of talking about the length of the race, we brought in the sprint concept. So, having an event starting from Friday, starting on Sunday, where every day there is something that in the sport the drivers have to fight for, that's the approach that we need to have. Listening, watching, trying to be progressive in anticipating certain things and not to be too static. Because to be static in this world, not only in our sport and platform, that means you go back."

Brian Sozzi is Yahoo Finance's Executive Editor and a member of Yahoo Finance's editorial leadership team. Follow Sozzi on X @BrianSozzi, Instagram, and LinkedIn. Tips on stories? Email brian.sozzi@yahoofinance.com.

title: BlueOval SK delays layoffs at Kentucky battery plant
content:
This story was originally published on WardsAuto. To receive daily news and insights, subscribe to our free daily WardsAuto newsletter.

Dive Brief:

Electric vehicle battery manufacturer BlueOval SK, the joint venture of Ford Motor Co. and SK On now undergoing dissolution, has delayed some of the planned layoffs at the manufacturing facility in Glendale, Kentucky, according to a Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification filed with the state on Feb 12. The layoffs of roughly 1,500 workers at the plant were originally expected to begin in a two-week period starting on Feb. 14, but the additional layoffs of around 10% of the plant's remaining workers is not expected to start until March 31, according to BlueOval SK filing. The winding down of operations at the Kentucky plant follows a December 2025 agreement reached between Ford and SK to dissolve their BlueOval SK joint venture due to lower than expected EV demand.

Dive Insight:

Roughly three dozen of the 150 retained workers at the Kentucky plant are listed as controls manufacturing process engineers and production supervisors, according to the WARN notice.

The BlueOval SK joint venture was established in September 2021 as part of a planned $11.4 billion investment by the two companies to build three large-scale manufacturing plants in the U.S., one in Tennessee and two in Kentucky, to produce advanced batteries for Ford’s future EVs.

The Kentucky plant started initial battery production in August 2025, according to a press release.

When the joint venture was announced, Ford said it was its largest manufacturing commitment in the company’s history. But with slowing demand, Ford does not need the additional battery production capacity it previously anticipated and announced in December that it was scaling back its EV plans.

As part of the agreement to end the joint venture between Ford and SK On, each company will independently own and operate the joint venture’s former production facilities, SK spokesperson Joe Guy Collier told WardsAuto in December. Collier said that a Ford subsidiary will take full ownership of the two battery plants in Kentucky, and SK On will assume full ownership and operate the plant in Tennessee.

The BlueOval SK Battery Park in Kentucky was originally slated to begin series production of advanced lithium-ion batteries late last year. But Ford announced plans last year to delay its EV launches in order to expand its hybrid vehicle offerings. However, Collier told WardsAuto in December that SK maintains its strategic relationship with Ford going forward, as the Tennessee plant that it’s taking ownership of is located within the automaker’s BlueOval City campus.

Story Continues

Ford, however, did not entirely abandon its EV plans. The automaker is currently working on a low-cost electric truck built on its new Universal Electric Vehicle platform. The midsize electric pickup is due to launch in 2027 with a target starting price of around $30,000. The vehicle will be built at Ford’s assembly plant in Louisville.

In addition to the job cuts in Kentucky, SK Battery America, which is a subsidiary of South Korea-based SK On, is also laying off hundreds of workers at its production facility in Commerce, Georgia. Earlier this month, The producer of nickel-cobalt-manganese lithium-ion battery cells filed a WARN notice in Georgia, notifying the state of layoffs impacting 958 workers at the plant.

SK Battery America also notified its workers in Georgia that they will receive pay and benefits for the next 60 days.

Ford in December also terminated its multi-billion dollar supply deal with South Korea-based LG Energy Solution for 75 gigawatt hours of battery cells for its electric vehicles in Europe. The value of the supply contract was worth 9.6 trillion won ($6.5 billion).

Correction: An earlier version of this story misstated the company that initiated the layoffs. The story has been updated to note it was BlueOval SK.
